ocr_snippet: Vitamin B12 (Cobalamin) is an important water-soluble vitamin, in contrast to other water-soluble vitamins it is not excreted quickly in the urine, but rather accumulates and is stored. As a result, a vitamin B12 deficiency may not manifest itself until 5 or 6 years of a diet supplying inadequate amounts. Vitamin B12 functions as a methyl donor and works with folic acid in the synthesis of DNA and red blood cells and is vitally important in the development of the myelin sheath...
